title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Recovery Case: Rebase Replay
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git rebase replay increment in a history-rewrite plan and verification graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Rebase Replay
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Rebase Replay contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.

content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Rebase Replay condition decided your prediction.

steps.title: Bound Rebase Replay
steps.instruction:
theory case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Rebase Replay application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.

content.text: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary. A defensible solution applies Rebase Replay, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

content.code:
git branch recovery/pre-rebase
git rebase --rebase-merges main
git range-diff recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
git diff recovery/pre-rebase^{tree} HEAD^{tree}
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Rebase moves existing commit objects without changing their IDs. This fails the Rebase Replay contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
